marketState: neutral-to-bearish: price sits at fair value but seasonality, storage and technical trend all skew lower
summary: Front is trading essentially FAIR ($2.940 vs fair $2.917, inside a wide $2.417-$3.417 band), so there is no valuation extreme to lean on right now, and the +7D/+14D/+21D fair-value projections are missing from this snapshot so the forward path can't be checked. Seasonality is the more useful signal: price is running -13.7% YTD vs a -3.4% median year (-10.3pp below typical), and the 4-week odds of a higher price are only 31%, though the model itself flags reliability as LOW (50pp spread) so this is a soft tilt, not a strong edge. Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average and still building (+61 Bcf last week), which caps rallies into the heart of cooling season. Technicals are bearish-trending on every timeframe from 15m to 1w (50<200 EMA on all six), but the daily is oversold (RSI 35.6, closing below the lower Bollinger Band) and 4h RSI is 24.9, so downside looks stretched short-term. Near-term weather (1-7D and 8-16D demand) data is missing entirely in this snapshot, which removes the best near-term catalyst check, so short-term conviction is capped.

longTerm.entry: $2.95 - $3.05 (scale in on a bounce toward curve resistance)
longTerm.rationale: Price is running well below its typical seasonal path (-13.7% YTD vs -3.4% median) with storage +184 Bcf above 5y and still building, both of which argue for continued softness into the fall shoulder; managed money is already net short but only at the 35th percentile of its 5y range, leaving room to add. Weekly technical trend is bearish (50<200 EMA), reinforcing the case, though the seasonality signal itself is flagged low-reliability so conviction stays modest.
longTerm.invalidation: A close back above ~$3.25 (above the Nov-contract curve hump and prior resistance), a swing to a surprise large storage draw/undersupply, or the seasonality/weather signals flipping decisively bullish would invalidate this

shortTerm.rationale: Every intraday-to-daily timeframe shows a bearish EMA structure, but the daily is already closing below its lower Bollinger Band with RSI at 35.6 (24.9 on 4h) — downside looks extended, not a fresh entry. Fair value shows front price as FAIR with no mispricing edge, and 1-7D/8-16D weather demand data is completely missing from this snapshot, so there is no near-term catalyst to confirm or deny a bounce.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a bounce into $3.05-$3.15 resistance (Oct/Nov curve levels) to sell with a tight stop, or a reset of daily/4h RSI out of oversold with weather demand data back online confirming direction, before taking a short-horizon position

Bearish 50<200 EMA trend structure confirmed on every technical timeframe from 15m to 1w
Managed money net short (-60,295 contracts) but only 35th percentile of 5y range, not extreme
Futures curve in steep contango into winter (Jan27 +44% over front) — normal seasonal risk premium, not a directional signal
risks: 1-7D and 8-16D weather demand data are both null in this snapshot — blind to any imminent heat-driven demand spike that could spark a sharp rally
